reasoning: With the end of Russian pipeline deliveries through Ukraine in January 2025, the EU relies on TurkStream and LNG for Russian gas. TurkStream's capacity to Europe is 15.75 bcm/year (~3.9 bcm/quarter). Western-facing Russian LNG facilities (Yamal, Portovaya, Vysotsk) have a maximum output of approximately 30 bcm/year (~7.5 bcm/quarter). Even if these operated at 100% capacity and sent every drop to the EU, total theoretical capacity is around 11.4 bcm/quarter. Without the resumption of the Ukraine transit or Yamal-Europe pipeline (both politically highly unlikely in 2025), physical infrastructure limits imports well below the 15 bcm threshold.
